overview: Affinity is an aggressive U/R deck that leverages artifact creatures to rapidly reduce the mana cost of its spells. By flooding the board with cheap artifacts, you enable highly efficient removal and finishers while applying relentless pressure. The game plan revolves around maximizing artifact count to trigger affinity reduction and overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Combat damage from a wide board of artifact creatures
Cheap, affinity-reduced finishers like Weapons Manufacturing
Token generation to accelerate artifact count and pressure

keep_criteria: Hands containing Mox Opal or other early ramp
Hands with Engineered Explosives or early removal
Hands with a clear path to turn 1 or 2 plays
Keep any hand with Mox Opal plus two artifacts, or three artifacts with a playable land.
Prioritize hands with Arcbound Ravager, Signal Pest, or turn-one artifact plays.
Aim for 4-5 artifact-producing/fixing lands (Shimmer Glen, Arcane Sanctum, Myr Meadow, Treetop Village) in your opening hand.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking artifact creatures or ramp
Hands with multiple removal spells but no threats
Mulligan hands with three or more lands, no artifacts, or only single artifacts without a land.

examples.reason: Provides turn 1 ramp, early creatures to reduce costs, and cheap removal.
Lacks artifact creatures and early ramp, making it impossible to trigger affinity reduction or apply early pressure.
Mox Opal, early mana rocks, and a turn-2 threat — Open deck list confirms opponent is a turn-4 combo deck, giving enough time to develop a fast clock.

General Affinity opening hand philosophy — Affinity does not mulligan well because it relies on having a full hand of cheap artifacts to litter the board. Mulliganing down to 4 cards leaves insufficient resources to function.

early_game: Play Mox Opal on turn 1 for mana acceleration
Cast 1-2 mana artifact creatures to build affinity
Use Engineered Explosives to clear early blockers or face-up threats
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Arcbound Ravager or Signal Pest.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast Mox Opal, or cast a second artifact.
If you have Opal and an artifact, tap Opal for mana to cast another threat or cast Engineered Explosives.
Sequence Opal as early as possible to enable explosive turns.
If opening with Opal, play it on turn 1 or 2 and use it to accelerate into your game plan.
mid_game: Flood the board with artifact creatures and tokens
Cast affinity-reduced threats and removal to maintain board control
Push damage consistently while protecting your board
Treat Mox Opal as your primary mana engine
tap it for one mana at a time to enable multiple spells per turn.
Avoid overcommitting artifacts if you lack protection or follow-up.
Use Engineered Explosives to clear boards when ahead or to stabilize when behind.
Manage your graveyard carefully if facing discard or graveyard hate, and prioritize keeping key artifacts in play.
Commit threats when you have Opal online to protect them or when you can follow up with removal.
late_game: Deploy high-impact finishers like Weapons Manufacturing at reduced cost
Use token generators to overwhelm opponent's removal
Close out the game with combat damage or direct damage spells
Arcbound Ravager combined with Mox Opal and Engineered Explosives for a lethal burst, or Weapons Manufacturing to generate a massive token army that overwhelms the board.
If the format allows, Glimpse the Unthinkable with an artifact in hand can close the game.
Otherwise, grind out value with artifact synergies and clear the board with EE to secure lethal.
Go all-in when you have Mox Opal online, the opponent has no interaction in hand, and you have a clear path to lethal.
Commit when you can protect your key artifacts or when Engineered Explosives can clear the board without hurting your own board.
Also commit when the opponent is on a combo turn you can interrupt with your interaction suite.

key_cards.name: Mox Opal
key_cards.notes: Provides early mana to cast artifact creatures and spells ahead of schedule. Requires an artifact creature in play to activate. | Play to generate extra mana, specifically to enable multiple actions in a turn (like casting a counter and activating a saga), overcoming mana bottlenecks. (from gameplay) | Provides essential red mana to cast Thought Monitor and accelerate artifact deployment, acting as a key enabler for the combo. (from gameplay)

key_cards.name: Engineered Explosives
key_cards.notes: Highly efficient removal that becomes extremely cheap with affinity. Use to clear face-up threats or protect your board. | Acts as a free artifact for Thoughtcast sequencing and can be cast for zero mana to deal massive damage when numerous Munitions tokens are on the board. (from gameplay)

key_cards.name: Weapons Manufacturing
key_cards.notes: Generates a large number of artifact creature tokens, rapidly increasing board presence and affinity reduction for subsequent spells. | Synergizes with Tormod's Crypt by creating a Munitions token when played into it, rapidly increasing the artifact count for combo finishes. (from gameplay) | Played to generate artifact tokens when casting Pinnacle Emissary, rapidly building artifact count to enable Kapsho Cannoner and Thought Monitor. (from gameplay)

sequencing_tips: Play artifact creatures before activating Mox Opal to enable mana acceleration
Cast Engineered Explosives before playing your next creature to maximize value and protect threats
Hold removal until after you see the opponent's response to avoid wasting mana
Prioritize playing creatures that generate tokens or reduce costs further
Use affinity reduction to cast finishers earlier than expected, catching opponents off guard
Against a known turn-4 combo deck, keep hands that can deploy a large threat on turn 2, as you typically have four turns to win before they can go off.
Hold back artifact hate cards until the opponent actually attempts their combo, rather than playing them immediately, to avoid giving them free turns to find an answer if they aren't going off yet.
When facing multiple Tormod's Crypts, Jeskai Blink's graveyard recursion plan becomes unrealistic, forcing a shift to a midrange game plan relying on Psychic Frog, Quantum Riddler, and Ephemerate.

Use Prismatic Ending on Engineered Explosives rather than Weapons Manufacturing if many Munitions tokens are already on the battlefield, as the tokens themselves will deal lethal damage when they leave.
Hold up Mystical Dispute to counter key enablers like Weapons Manufacturing or Pinnacle Emissary to slow down Affinity's explosive turns and buy time for answers.
Hold Engineered Explosives until you are winning; casting it early removes too many 0-cost artifacts needed for combo pieces. Only use it as a desperation play to kill a key threat like Lotus Bloom.
Avoid tapping your last mana source if you need to represent interaction like Metallic Rebuke, as it leaves you vulnerable to the opponent's plays.
Common mistakes include overcommitting artifacts without Opal protection, forgetting to tap Opal for mana, and mismanaging Engineered Explosives targets.
Always track the opponent's hand for counterspells and sweepers.

matchups.advice: Apply maximum early pressure and play around counterspells. Flood the board with tokens to overwhelm removal.
Race them by pushing damage early. Use Engineered Explosives to clear their key threats before they stabilize.
Use cheap removal to clear their early threats and stabilize with artifact creatures. Race with token generation.

Prioritize sideboarding permission alongside pressure cards to force the opponent to use their counters on threats rather than their combo pieces. (from gameplay) Lead with Öza's Saga on turn one to enable casting Pithing Needle by turn three, naming Goblin Charbelcher to shut down the combo. (from gameplay) Use Torment of Hailfire to interact with the Tameshi, Golden Spirit side of the combo, as it won't stop Goblin Charbelcher itself but disrupts the mana engine. (from gameplay) If Amulet Titan resolves Goblin Charbelcher through Mystical Dispute, they cannot activate it immediately, buying crucial tempo to find answers. (from gameplay)

Include three main deck Tormod's Crypts to disrupt their graveyard recursion strategy; the low mana cost of Affinity's zero-mana artifacts makes this inclusion cost-effective. (from gameplay) Engineered Explosives can serve as a combo kill finish when cast for zero mana, dealing two damage per Munitions token that leaves the battlefield. (from gameplay) Consigned to Memory is effective against colorless strategies but does not counter Pinnacle Emissary since it is a creature, so plan removal accordingly. (from gameplay)

Cast spells in response to counterspells, use Mox Opal to protect key artifacts, and rely on Engineered Explosives to clear the board if your threats are countered. Use cheap artifacts to pressure through interaction, and consider casting spells when the opponent has no mana available.

Apply early pressure to prevent them from stabilizing. Use Engineered Explosives to clear ramp threats before they can cast their win conditions. Do not let them get ahead in mana; trade artifacts efficiently and use your interaction suite to disrupt their key spells.

Win on card advantage and better sequencing. Use Flusterstorm to protect your key spells and disrupt theirs. Use Engineered Explosives to clear boards favorably. Trade artifacts efficiently, and prioritize keeping Mox Opal online. Do not overcommit to the board; wait for the opponent to make a mistake or run out of resources.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (Flusterstorm, Spell Pierce, Mana Leak), board wipes (Swords to Plowshares, Wrath of God, Engineered Explosives), and artifact hate (Rest in Peace, Null Rod, Surgical Extraction, Leyline of Punishment).
Track the opponent's hand size and graveyard to anticipate combo turns or sweepers.
Note when they tap out for interaction, as this is your window to attack or cast key spells.
